title: Direct behavior review parses real test calls and includes existing-test assertion deltas
context: The first modified-test extractor correctly found assertion deltas but its legacy added-name scan also interpreted test-like text inside string literals as registered tests. Exact review must never mint a candidate from quoted documentation or fixture source.
decision: For an exact current human-confirmed fixing decision, direct behavior review derives both newly named and modified test candidates only from parsed literal node:test test()/it() call expressions. A modified case additionally requires a fixing-diff added line within that exact call range. Candidate identity remains bound to the decision, fixing commit, file, literal name, full known-good source hash, and grounding mode.
consequences: Assertions added to existing named tests enter the exact replay loop.
Quoted or commented test-like text never becomes a candidate.
Legacy direct candidates representing real test calls retain their identities; replay and activation rules are unchanged.
alternatives_rejected: Keep regex discovery for newly named direct tests: rejected because test-like fixture strings create fabricated candidates.
Treat every changed line in a test file as a candidate: rejected because it cannot bind a particular executable behavior.
Require helper-symbol existence: rejected because implementation structure is not a durable behavioral proof.
